title: sedum cockerellii
intro: Sedum cockerellii, also known as Cockerell's stonecrop, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the family Crassulaceae. It is commonly found in the southwestern United States and Mexico, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Sedum cockerellii include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and act as an antiseptic. Sedum cockerellii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
